summary: Completed read-only audit of DinoBrain retrieval quality against RAG best practices. Verdicts: contextual chunks PASS at basic source-chunk/metadata-context level; sparse+dense hybrid FAIL for current default because live retrieval is lexical_fallback_v2 and no dense vector index is present; reranking PASS because BM25/RRF/provenance/root/session reranking is implemented and visible in context-pack reasons; operation-log pollution FAIL because 60_Operations and task-summary records are present in the current index and operation records can dominate operation-flavored searches; current default retrieval for the user's LLM Wiki goal FAIL until dense vectors/index freshness/noise gates are fixed, though it does retrieve the intended LLM Wiki goal records for this audit query. No source files were edited by this audit.
decisions: Use live Context Pack and local code/data evidence as source of truth.
Do not run npm/node verifier scripts because node is unavailable on the shell PATH.
Treat operation-log pollution as a retrieval-quality failure because 60_Operations records dominate operation-flavored ranked output and task-summary paths remain in the current wiki index.
next_steps: Add or configure a real dense vector index/provider and gate claims on hybrid_contextual_v2 when required.
Rebuild or invalidate stale wiki indexes so task summaries excluded by current code are not still present in persisted index files.
Separate operational reports/logs from default user-memory retrieval, or require explicit operation intent before ranking 60_Operations records highly.
search_queries: RAG verification subagent task summary operation log
